business.headline: India-US trade deal framework ready as talks progress amid tariff uncertaintyIn February, the two sides agreed to 18% tariffs on Indian goods in exchange for New Delhi lowering trade barriers and buying more American goods. But a final deal has been clouded by the U.S. Supreme Court ruling invalidating President Donald Trump's sweeping global tariffs.

About 2 crore ITRs filed so far. Should you wait for a deadline extension?With around two crore returns already filed and the filing process running smoothly this year, taxpayers may be better off completing their returns well before the deadline.

From Rs 5,000 late fee to 200% penalty: Income tax mistakes that could cost you bigA small mistake in your income tax compliance can turn into a big financial setback. From late filing fees to steep penalties for under-reporting income, the Income Tax Act prescribes strict consequences for different types of defaults. Are you aware of the most common ones?

June retail inflation climbs to 4.38%, breaches RBI's 4% target after 5 monthsThe latest reading marks the first time since January 2026 that headline inflation has crossed the RBI's 4% target. Retail inflation had eased to 2.74% in January before gradually rising to 3.21% in February, 3.40% in March, 3.48% in April and 3.93% in May.

Piyush Goyal dismisses report claiming India is holding back on US trade dealThe rebuttal comes at a sensitive stage in India-US trade negotiations, with both sides racing to bridge differences on tariffs and market access ahead of a proposed bilateral trade agreement.

Why SK Hynix shares crashed over 15% days after their Nasdaq debutJust days after making a strong start in the US market, the South Korean chipmaker's shares plunged more than 15% on Monday, as investors rushed to book profits.

Delhi's property boom is creating an unusual courtroom problemWhat do soaring home prices have to do with courtrooms? According to the Delhi High Court, more than you might think. Here's the unusual problem now under scrutiny.

Why is crude oil back on the boil?Higher crude oil prices increase the country's import bill and can put pressure on inflation if fuel and transportation costs rise. They can also weaken the rupee as oil marketing companies require more US dollars to pay for imports.

Why TCS, Infosys and HCLTech shares rose despite a weak marketShares of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys, HCLTech and LTIMindtree rose in early trade, outperforming the broader market and signalling renewed investor interest in a sector that has been under pressure for months.

What's behind India's tougher stance in trade talks with USThe US entered trade talks from a position of strength. But a series of developments may be tilting the balance towards New Delhi. Here's why India is in no hurry to strike a deal.

Sensex falls over 300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Indian benchmark indices fell sharply after renewed US-Iran hostilities lifted crude oil concerns. The sell-off reflected worries over India's import bill, inflation and the market impact if Brent climbs above $90.

Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IndiGo down 3%The BSE Sensex fell 626.40 points, or 0.81%, to 76,942.99, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 184 points, or 0.76%, to 24,022.90 as of 9:25 am.

A joint loan, a separation and a damaged credit score: Her story is a warningShe wasn't the one who defaulted, yet her loan application was affected. He never borrowed the money, yet he had to repay the debt. These two real-life stories reveal the hidden risks of saying 'yes' to someone else's loan. Could the same happen to you?

Swiggy Instamart receives 9 notices from food regulator over quality complaintsFSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints about expired, spoiled and contaminated food deliveries. The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Apple sues OpenAI, ex-employees over alleged trade secrets theftApple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misuse of trade secrets for consumer hardware. The case sharpens tensions between partners as both companies push deeper into AI devices.

RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

entertainment.headline: Korean Kanakaraju teaser: Possessed Varun Tej takes on Korean mafia The makers of Korean Kanakaraju have unveiled the official teaser and fixed its theatrical release for August 7. The glimpse sets up Varun Tej in two contrasting shades while linking Rayalaseema, the supernatural and Korean gangster lore.

Lock Upp's Akanksha Chamola, earlier identified as bisexual, now says she's asexual Lock Upp contestant Akanksha Chamola, who earlier revealed she was bisexual before marriage, has now said she identifies as asexual. The actor explained that sexuality can evolve with different phases of life and linked her current feelings to her ongoing divorce.

Amitabh Bachchan says unfair decisions at FIFA World Cup 2026 leave him distressed Amitabh Bachchan said some decisions at the 2026 FIFA World Cup left him "distressed" as he shared his thoughts on the tournament in his latest blog. The actor also reflected on national pride, teamwork and the changing times.

Avengers Doomsday concept art: First look at RDJ's Doctor Doom, Loki and Beast Former Marvel visual development director Andy Park has shared Avengers: Doomsday concept art showing Robert Downey Jr's Doctor Doom mask, Loki and the returning X-Men. The artwork marks Park's final full film at Marvel Studios and offers an early look at characters not yet seen in promotional material.

The Marvelous Mrs Maisel actor, Broadway star Josh Grisetti dies by suicide at 44 Broadway actor and The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel star Josh Grisetti has died by suicide at the age of 44. His longtime friend and fellow actor Rob McClure confirmed the news, calling his death a "cataclysmic loss."

Ramayana heads to China: All about Ranbir Kapoor and Yash's ambitious marketing push Ramayana: Part 1 is reportedly planning a major promotional campaign in China, with Ranbir Kapoor, Yash and Sai Pallavi expected to lead a multi-city tour. The move comes as the makers eye a strong release in the world's second-largest theatrical market.

Satluj actor Varun Badola reacts to Diljit urging fans to download film and watch Actor Varun Badola has said he cannot support piracy amid the Satluj controversy and Diljit Dosanjh's call to circulate copies. His remarks come as the film remains blocked, screenings continue elsewhere, and the dispute deepens over access and censorship.

Censor board didn't ask any cuts: The India Story director reacts to legal notice The India Story director Chettan DK has denied reports that the CBFC asked for cuts to the film, saying the team only improvised a few scenes. His remarks come as the Kajal Aggarwal-Shreyas Talpade starrer faces a legal notice over its promotional material.

Digger trailer: An eccentric, unrecognisable Tom Cruise finds new impossible mission Actor Tom Cruise appears in an eccentric new avatar as oil baron Digger Rockwell in the first trailer for Digger. The dark comedy places him at the centre of a disaster of his own making as he races to contain the fallout.

Imtiaz Ali shares daughter Ida's wedding plans after proposal pics go viral Filmmaker Imtiaz Ali has said his daughter Ida Ali's engagement to Krish Agarwal is the greatest happiness of his life. The family says there is no rush for a wedding, with the couple choosing to enjoy the moment.

Ranveer's Rs 3,000-crore Dhurandhar is box office dud in Japan, draws 900 viewers Despite the Dhurandhar franchise earning over Rs 3,100 crore worldwide, its Japan debut has failed to impress. The Ranveer Singh-starrer managed only around 900 viewers on its opening day despite releasing across nearly 80 screens.

Indian Muslims have already proved their patriotism: Imtiaz Ali to critics Imtiaz Ali has defended Main Vaapas Aaunga against anti-national criticism, saying it focusses on Partition's human cost. He said the film rejects hatred and reflects on belonging, displacement and patriotism.

Can Deepika Padukone play Hema Malini in her biopic? What Dream Girl says At a celebration of her 60 years in cinema, actor Hema Malini said Deepika Padukone could play her in a biopic. The remark revived their long association, from Hema's biography launch to the Dream Girl echo in Om Shanti Om.

Paraak: Sriimurali's teaser delivers Kannada cinema's most stylish action teaser The makers of Paraak unveiled the film's teaser, introducing Sriimurali's next action entertainer after Bagheera. Its grounded combat staging and Sriimurali's transformed look quickly drove online discussion.

Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya trailer: Kay Kay Menon leads a chaotic school to Cambridge The trailer for Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya follows actor Kay Kay Menon's principal Gyaneshwar Tripathi as he pushes his chaotic school towards better board results and a Cambridge training opportunity. The series sets up a comedy-drama about ambition, limited resources and the daily disorder of school life.

After slamming paps for being intrusive, Neha Dhupia says they respect her privacy In an interview with us, actor Neha Dhupia reflected on her equation with the paparazzi, days after her confrontation at an event. She discussed how they have been supportive of her work.

Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi to release in 2027? Here's what we know Salman Khan's film Maatrubhumi faces a possible delay until 2027. The makers are hopeful for a Dussehra release if issues are resolved.

Made in India fame Lakshvir Saran says Naseeruddin Shah's praise is beyond awards In an exclusive conversation with India Today, actor Lakshvir Singh Saran, who was seen in Made in India: A Titan Story, reflects on building a real-life character with minimal references, the power of subtle romance, and why honest, inspiring storytelling matters in Indian streaming.

How a 6-year-old's cancer journey inspired The India Story's controversial subject The India Story director Chettan DK has shared that the film was inspired by the cancer diagnosis of a six-year-old child. His comments come as the Kajal Aggarwal-Shreyas Talpade starrer faces legal scrutiny ahead of its release.

general news.headline: Trump reimposes Iran blockade, seeks 20% fee on all cargo shipped through Hormuz Live updatesDonald Trump went on to announce that the US would become the "Guardian of the Hormuz Strait" and said countries using the shipping lane should bear the cost of its protection.

No paid crowd: PM's Melbourne event organisers seek Rahul Gandhi, Kharge apology'Melbourne Meets Modi' event organisers rejected Congress leaders' claims that attendees were a 'paid crowd', asserting that the nearly 30,000 participants attended voluntarily at their own or community-supported expense.

53% of NDA voters don't prefer E20 petrol, 50% fear vehicle damage: C-Voter surveyA C-Voter survey finds more than half of NDA voters unwilling to use E20 petrol, with widespread fears over vehicle damage and lower mileage. The findings reveal why the Centre's ethanol push continues to face resistance.

CM Vijay shunts vigilance chief after 2 months amid row over his appointmentVijay removed A Arun as Vigilance and Anti-Corruption chief less than two months after his appointment, amid lingering controversy and judicial scrutiny over his appointment to the post.

Watch: US releases video of its 1st-ever suicide drone attack on Iranian submarineUS forces struck a submarine and ship maintenance facility at Iran's Bandar Abbas Naval Base using one-way attack sea drones. The operation, followed by Trump's fresh Hormuz claims and Iran's rejection, sharpened regional tensions.

Flight carrying bodies of 15 Indians killed in Vietnam boat tragedy lands in MumbaiFlight VN 979 from Ho Chi Minh City touched down at 9.19 pm, carrying the mortal remains of the victims. Family members and officials were present as arrangements began to transport the bodies to their respective hometowns for the last rites, according to PTI.

Odisha man dies days after wife hits him on head with phone during argumentThe family has alleged that the head injury from the June 21 domestic dispute eventually led to his death. However, police have said the exact cause of death will be established only after the post-mortem examination.

Railways land equal to 42 Narendra Modi cricket stadiums under encroachment: RTIAn RTI response from the Railway Board has revealed the scale of Indian Railways' encroachment challenge: 1,068.54 hectares of its land under illegal occupation as of March 2025. The figures reveal a worrying trend: encroachments have grown over the years and the Railways lacks a comprehensive long-term database of the problem.

iPhone 18 Pro launching in 2 months: India price, launch date, specifications and moreApple's next flagship is just around the corner. From a bigger battery and faster A20 Pro chip to camera upgrades, a smaller Dynamic Island and possible price hikes, here's everything leaks and reports have revealed about the iPhone 18 Pro ahead of its expected September launch.

How a war turned Argentina vs England football into India-Pak-like showdownA war over a remote South Atlantic archipelago turned Argentina vs England into football's closest equivalent of the India-Pakistan cricket rivalry. From Maradona's brilliance to modern-day controversies, every meeting between the Three Lions and the Albiceleste carries the weight of history, national pride and football.

Trump backs J&K statehood? Omar Abdullah shares satirical post to mock BJPJammu and Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah shared a satirical fake Donald Trump post to attack the BJP over the restoration of Jammu and Kashmir's statehood. The post came as the National Conference prepared to intensify its protests against the BJP at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar.

Ex-AAP leader Tahir Hussain convicted of murdering IB officer during Delhi riotsA Delhi court has delivered a major verdict in the 2020 riots case, convicting former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain and four others while acquitting six accused. The court's ruling also brought emotional scenes inside the courtroom.

Women are meant to nurture: Kangana backs Anandiben Patel's 'learn to cook' remarkKangana Ranaut backed Uttar Pradesh Governor Anandiben Patel's remarks urging women to learn to cook for their families. Her endorsement drew criticism online and widened the debate over gender roles.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup winAustralia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

Live: US forces hit Iranian sub, ship maintenance facility, release video of strikesUS-Iran War Live Updates: Fighting between the United States and Iran has intensified sharply, with both sides exchanging fresh missile and drone strikes on Monday. Iran says it has once again closed the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, while the US insists the international waterway remains open to commercial shipping. The latest attacks have reached Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Jordan, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain, raising fears of a wider regional conflict. Washington has carried out fresh strikes on Iranian military targets around Hormuz, while Tehran has targeted US-linked military facilities across the Gulf and vowed to defend the key shipping route. The renewed violence has cast serious doubt on last month's interim US-Iran agreement, which was intended to reopen the strait and pave the way for a broader peace deal after 60 days of negotiations. President Donald Trump has declared the ceasefire effectively over, even as he says he remains open to talks. The escalating conflict has also disrupted one of the world's busiest energy corridors, pushing up oil prices and raising concerns over global trade. Iran has warned ships not to enter the strait without its approval, while the US says its forces are positioned to protect freedom of navigation. As military operations continue, Gulf nations are dealing with missile and drone attacks, civilian injuries and damage to critical infrastructure, while diplomatic efforts led by Oman, Qatar and other regional partners struggle to keep pace. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest developments, key statements and real-time analysis from the rapidly evolving US-Iran conflict.

Arshad Nadeem confirms participation in CWG 2026, set to face Neeraj ChopraThe Neeraj Chopra-Arshad Nadeem rivalry is set for another chapter after the Pakistan star confirmed his participation in the Commonwealth Games 2026. Nadeem will defend the gold medal he won in Birmingham, while Chopra returns to the Games after missing the previous edition through injury.

Mother and son graduate together from IIT Madras at 45 and 21At the IIT Madras convocation, Jigisha Tailor and her son Aditya Kapadia received degrees from the same online data science programme together. The unexpected moment capped a journey of shared study, family support and friendly competition.

El Nino shows true colours, makes India's monsoon gasp for rain cloudsIndia's monsoon has entered a fresh break phase as El Nino suppresses rain-bearing systems. Forecasters expect below-normal rainfall for now, with a possible revival later in July.

History at Home of Cricket: India win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sIt took more than 140 years for Lord's to host its first-ever women's Test. India made the wait worthwhile, hammering England by 270 runs. Yastika Bhatia's century, Smriti Mandhana's twin fifties and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul powered a landmark victory at the Home of Cricket.

The next oil shock won't shake India like in the past. Story of a 90-day shiftThe Iran war delivered a jolt to India's energy security, exposing the risks of heavy reliance on the Middle East. Even as the conflict gets reignited after US strikes, India's state-run refiners seem to be better-positioned to deal with geopolitical disruptions. Here's how a shift took place in 90 days.

Man quits corporate career after 18 years, says office politics cost him promotionDharmendra Pandey, who describes himself in his Instagram bio as someone "helping working professionals build an income outside their job," posted a video reflecting on his nearly two decades in the corporate world and the lessons he learnt before quitting.

15 lakh ineligible Ladli Behna applicants found, no mass deletions: MinisterThe Maharashtra government dismissed reports of mass deletions from the Ladli Behna Yojana, saying a statewide verification drive has identified around 15 lakh ineligible applicants while ensuring no eligible woman is excluded.

Thousands of dog-owners had to put pets to death in Punjab; a terrorism horror storyDiljit Dosanjh's Satluj has brought the dangerous days of terrorism in Punjab into the spotlight. One well-documented aspect of those days is the massacre of thousands of dogs across the state. At a time when we are debating how best or kindly to deal with street dogs, it is instructive to read this horror story about why Punjab's dogs became collateral damage.

Is Physics the toughest Olympiad? India's five-gold triumph makes the caseIndia's clean sweep at the 56th International Physics Olympiad is more than a historic achievement. It makes a compelling case for the Physics Olympiad as one of the world's toughest academic competitions, where success demands not just mastery of theory but exceptional experimental skill and scientific thinking.

sports.headline: Arshad Nadeem confirms participation in CWG 2026, set to face Neeraj Chopra The Neeraj Chopra-Arshad Nadeem rivalry is set for another chapter after the Pakistan star confirmed his participation in the Commonwealth Games 2026. Nadeem will defend the gold medal he won in Birmingham, while Chopra returns to the Games after missing the previous edition through injury.

RCB coach Andy Flower will not leave IPL to join England Test team: Karthik RCB head coach Andy Flower is one of the early favourites to become the head coach of the England Test team. However, former India cricketer, Dinesh Karthik believes that there is one small problem which would stop the coach from switching sides to England.

Brendon McCullum breaks silence after sacking from England Test coach role Brendon McCullum said he was disappointed after losing the England Test job and apologised to supporters. He said that Bazball changed the side's identity, but failures in the Ashes and against India proved decisive.

Harmanpreet calls for more women's Test matches after historic win at Lord's India captain Harmanpreet Kaur has called for more women's Test matches after leading her side to a historic 270-run victory over England at Lord's. Speaking after India's massive win, she hoped that the team gets "ten more Test matches" to continue proving themselves in the longest format.

Loved Sooryavanshi's Wimbledon look? Decoding his Centre Court style game Liked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's sharp look at the All England Club? From his structured black suit and striped club tie to those sleek leather loafers, we decode the teenage batting prodigy's effortless, last-minute Wimbledon style game.

Retirement far off, Kohli helping India plan for ODI World Cup 2027 India captain Shubman Gill has revealed that former India captain Virat Kohli is heavily involved in the planning of ODI World Cup 2027. Gill also answered queries about Rohit Sharma and his place in the team going into the marquee tournament.

History at Home of Cricket: India win first-ever women's Test at Lord's It took more than 140 years for Lord's to host its first-ever women's Test. India made the wait worthwhile, hammering England by 270 runs. Yastika Bhatia's century, Smriti Mandhana's twin fifties and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul powered a landmark victory at the Home of Cricket.

Sachin drops in at Lord's, leads pep talk as India women chase historic win Sachin Tendulkar paid a surprise visit to the Indian women's team at Lord's ahead of the fourth day of the historic one-off Test against England. He was seen leading the talk to the team as Harmanpreet Kaur's side closed in on a famous victory.

Day 4 Highlights: India win by 270 runs, win first-ever women's Test at Lord's England (ENG) vs India (IND) Day 4 Highlights, women's one-off Test: Harmanpreet Kaur's India win the first ever women's Test match at Lord's. India beat England by a massive margin of 270 runs.

After dashing Wimbledon appearance, Sanju Samson reveals Novak Djokovic inspiration At Wimbledon, Sanju Samson said Novak Djokovic's discipline and demeanour remain a benchmark for athletes. Yuvraj Singh backed that view, highlighting Djokovic's self-belief, longevity and influence on younger players across the globe.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup win Australia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

How Sachin Tendulkar is backing a quiet sporting revolution in BastarSachin Tendulkar visited Dantewada to support Bastar's Maidan Cup and meet village children. The initiative is expanding community playgrounds and restoring confidence through sport in a region shaped by insurgency.

Shreyas Iyer's interview over Sooryavanshi snub irks ex-captain: Not the way to talkKris Srikkanth criticised Shreyas Iyer and the team management for dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Southampton T20I. He said the move and Iyer's remarks showed poor handling of a young player.

IPL: Stephen Fleming steps down as CSK head coach after 17 years at helmIPL: Chennai Super Kings parted ways with head coach Stephen Fleming after an 18-year association, drawing the curtains on the IPL's most successful coaching era. CSK finished three consecutive seasons outside the top four.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken?India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Andy Flower took England to No.1: Nasser backs RCB coach to replace McCullumFormer England captain Nasser Hussain has backed Andy Flower to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test head coach, saying his meticulous approach is exactly what the team needs after the Bazball era.

Zverev aims to move beyond tennis' third guy tag, close gap on Sinner and AlcarazWimbledon runner-up Alexander Zverev will return to world No. 2 on Monday, but believes his attacking game has finally brought him closer to Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz after years of being labelled tennis' "third guy".

Not at full throttle, Jannik Sinner still stands atop the tennis worldJannik Sinner proved that true champions are not defined only by dominance but by their ability to survive adversity and find a way to win. On Sunday, the Italian showcased exactly that, defending his Wimbledon title with a hard-fought victory over Alexander Zverev in the final.

Yastika Bhatia reveals how Mandhana's advice fuelled her Lord's comeback centuryINDW vs ENGW, one-off Test: Yastika Bhatia credited Smriti Mandhana's advice for helping her overcome a serious knee injury after marking her comeback with a maiden international century at Lord's.

The Bazball verdict: Brilliant at home, exposed by the eliteBrendon McCullum's Bazball promised to revolutionise England's Test cricket. Four years later, sacked after a humiliating Ashes defeat, the numbers reveal the truth: it worked at home and against weak teams. Against India and Australia, it crumbled.

Lord's Test: Yastika Bhatia, Kranti Gaud leave India on verge of famous win at Day 3Yastika Bhatia became the first woman to score a Test hundred at Lord's before Kranti Gaud's fiery spell left England six down as India took complete control on Day 3 of the historic Women's Test, setting the hosts a daunting 457-run target.

Alexander Zverev's runner-up finish fuels belief he can one day win WimbledonAlexander Zverev said reaching his maiden Wimbledon final has convinced him he can one day lift the title despite losing to Jannik Sinner. The German praised the world No. 1 while reflecting on a breakthrough fortnight at the All England Club.

Ice-cool Jannik Sinner fights off feisty Alexander Zverev to retain crownWorld No.1 Jannik Sinner came from a set down to beat Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 and successfully defend his Wimbledon title on Sunday, July 12 on Centre Court.

Rahul Dravid among candidates to replace Brendon McCullum as England Test coachRahul Dravid has reportedly emerged as a contender to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test coach. The former India coach features on a shortlist that includes Andy Flower and Richard Dawson, though the report says he is reluctant to coach full-time.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes Wimbledon debut, picks favourite tennis starTeen cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi enjoyed his first Wimbledon visit, attending the men's singles final alongside Yuvraj Singh, Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson. The 15-year-old also revealed his admiration for Novak Djokovic and backed Jannik Sinner.

Wimbledon Highlights: Sinner crowned champion again, beat Zverev in finalWimbledon Final Highlights: Jannik Sinner has successfully defended his Wimbledon crown after beating Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 in three hours and 46 minutes on Centre Court.

End of Bazball as McCullum sacked as Test coach after Ben Stokes retirementBrendon McCullum has been sacked as England's Test head coach, just days after Ben Stokes announced his retirement from international cricket. However, the former New Zealand captain will continue in his role as head coach of England's ODI and T20I sides.

Yastika Bhatia becomes first woman to hit Test century at Lord'sEngland vs India, only Test: Yastika Bhatia became the first batter to score a century in a women's Test at Lord's Cricket Ground as India dominated proceedings on Day 3 of the iconic match.

FIFA will explore expanding World Cup to 64 teams before 2030 edition: InfantinoFIFA president confirmed discussions for a 64-team tournament will take place ahead of the 2030 centenary edition, insisting football's global showpiece must look beyond the traditional dominance of Europe and South America.

India tour of England, Zimbabwe: Prince and Bishnoi named injury replacementsIndia have brought in Prince Yadav and Ravi Bishnoi after Harshit Rana and Varun Chakaravarthy suffered hamstring injuries during the recently-concluded England T20Is. The reshuffle comes ahead of the ODIs in England and the T20Is in Zimbabwe.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
